title: Add default_log_path config option for all container operations
body:
Introduces a new default_log_path configuration option that provides automatic log path assignment for both podman run and podman kube play operations. This setting works with all file-based log drivers and is only applied when no explicit --log-opt path= is provided, giving users better control over container log organization without requiring manual path specification for each command.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
Yes

```release-note
Added `default_log_path` configuration option for automatic log path assignment. When configured, this option automatically sets log paths for containers using file-based log drivers (journald, k8s-file, json-file) when no explicit `--log-opt path=` is specified, providing better control over container log organization without requiring manual path specification for each command.
action required
```

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Good question! The existing `log_path` implementation (commit 930cd25) provides a fixed log path for **all** containers, but our `default_log_path` serves a different purpose:

**Key differences:**

1. **Existing `log_path`**: Sets the same log directory for all containers. When set, all containers log to subdirectories under this path (e.g., `/var/log/containers/{container-id}/ctr.log`)

2. **Our `default_log_path`**: Provides a **default relative path** that containers can use when no explicit `--log-opt path=` is specified. This gives users more flexible control over individual container log locations.

**Use case distinction:**
- `log_path`: "I want all container logs centralized under `/var/log/containers/`"
- `default_log_path`: "When I don't specify a path, use `logs/myapp.log` as the default, but I can still override with `--log-opt path=` for specific containers"

The `default_log_path` also includes security validation (prevents absolute paths and directory traversal) and only applies when no explicit path is provided, making it more suitable for cases where users want a reasonable default but still need per-container customization.

Would you like me to update the PR description to clarify this distinction?
Actually feel free to drop it if you don't like it - I was assigned to RHEL-3114 so was thinking I need to implement such mechanism which is currently missing.

Using the same path for every container makes no sense because then the logs of many containers end up in one file making it impossible for podman logs to show the container logs.

As far as I am concerned log_path is the solution we want with for RHEL-3114, I guess the card simply was not moved to code review/done when Joshua left. cc @mheon
Yeah, sorry @jnovy - I moved the card for packaging/QE work assuming it already had a link to the PR that implemented. What I get for not checking before reassigning.
